title: myrtus rigida
intro: Myrtus rigida, also known as the stiff-leaved myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving coughs and colds, reducing fever, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Myrtus rigida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
